problem_statement: The live adapter proof used a bounded Gmail connector token, but durable Gmail runtime access needs a governed OAuth lifecycle before long-lived production use.
success_criteria: least-privilege Gmail scopes are selected before any OAuth client or consent-screen mutation
testing and production Google Cloud project boundaries are documented before verification submission
refresh-token storage, rotation, revocation, redaction, and receipt evidence are specified before runtime binding
send and draft actions remain approval-gated and are not admitted by a read-only connector token
repository validators reject overclaiming durable OAuth readiness without provider-side evidence
non_goals: no Google Cloud credential creation in this change
no OAuth consent-screen publication in this change
no production verification submission in this change
no secret value storage in repository artifacts
no expansion from Gmail to Google Calendar or Microsoft Graph in this change
constraints: Gmail scope selection must prefer the narrowest documented scope that satisfies the operation
Sensitive or restricted Google scopes require explicit verification evidence before public production use
Repository receipts must record secret presence only, never token or refresh-token values
External-account mutations require operator authorization and a fresh witness
Durable runtime access must include revocation, rotation, expiration, and failed-refresh paths
affected_surfaces: Google Cloud OAuth consent and credentials
evidence_required: Google OAuth client creation witness without secret serialization
approval-gated send or draft receipt when write operations are enabled
